examples.title: Converting E-R to Relational Schema
examples.description: Algorithm to convert E-R model entities and relationships to database tables
examples.code:
// ENTITY CONVERSION
FUNCTION ConvertEntityToTable(Entity E)
BEGIN
  CREATE TABLE E.EntityName
  FOR EACH attribute a IN E.attributes
    ADD COLUMN a.name a.dataType
  IF E has composite attributes THEN
    FOR EACH composite a IN E.compositeAttributes
      ADD all simple sub-attributes as columns
  IF E has multivalued attribute m THEN
    CREATE TABLE EntityName_MultivaluedAttr
      ADD PRIMARY KEY (Entity_Key, Multivalued_Attr_ID)
      ADD FOREIGN KEY REFERENCES Entity
END

// RELATIONSHIP CONVERSION  
FUNCTION ConvertRelationshipToTable(Relationship R)
BEGIN
  IF cardinality = 1:1 THEN
    ADD foreign key to either entity table
  ELSE IF cardinality = 1:N THEN
    ADD foreign key to 'N' side entity table
  ELSE IF cardinality = M:N THEN
    CREATE TABLE RelationshipName
      ADD foreign keys to all participating entities
      ADD primary key as combination of foreign keys
      ADD relationship attributes if any
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how to transform E-R diagram components into relational database schema. Entities become tables, attributes become columns, and relationships are converted based on cardinality type.

examples.title: E-R Model Python Class Representation
examples.description: Python classes to represent E-R model components with attributes
examples.code:
class Entity:
    def __init__(self, name, is_weak=False):
        self.name = name
        self.is_weak = is_weak
        self.attributes = {}
        self.primary_key = None
    
    def add_attribute(self, attr_name, attr_type, is_key=False, 
                      is_composite=False, is_multivalued=False, is_derived=False):
        self.attributes[attr_name] = {
            'type': attr_type,
            'is_key': is_key,
            'is_composite': is_composite,
            'is_multivalued': is_multivalued,
            'is_derived': is_derived
        }
        if is_key:
            self.primary_key = attr_name

class Relationship:
    def __init__(self, name, participating_entities, cardinality):
        self.name = name
        self.participants = participating_entities  # List of Entity objects
        self.cardinality = cardinality  # '1:1', '1:N', 'M:N'
        self.participation = {}  # 'total' or 'partial' for each entity
        self.attributes = {}

# Example: Create Student entity
student = Entity("Student", is_weak=False)
student.add_attribute("Student_ID", "VARCHAR(10)", is_key=True)
student.add_attribute("Name", "VARCHAR(100)", is_composite=True)
student.add_attribute("Age", "INT", is_derived=True)
student.add_attribute("Emails", "VARCHAR(100)", is_multivalued=True)

# Create Course entity  
course = Entity("Course", is_weak=False)
course.add_attribute("Course_ID", "VARCHAR(10)", is_key=True)
course.add_attribute("Course_Name", "VARCHAR(50)")
course.add_attribute("Credits", "INT")

# Create Enrolls relationship (M:N)
enrolls = Relationship("Enrolls", [student, course], "M:N")
enrolls.attributes["Grade"] = "CHAR(2)"
enrolls.attributes["Semester"] = "VARCHAR(20)"
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates object-oriented representation of E-R model components. It defines Entity and Relationship classes with support for all attribute types, cardinalities, and participation constraints.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n=number of entities and relationships created
